summary: The CEREC AC likely uses some kind of light technology for its operations but does not explicitly utilize traditional dental lasers such as those found in dermabrasion treatments; it may involve invisible wavelengths more suited towards digital scanning rather than material cutting directly during restoration processes. Agent1 concludes based largely upon indirect associations between various types of optical tools employed within modern restorative practices without concrete proof linking specific models to exact
